content_hints.description: The stairs crumble beneath your feet as you descend, each step older and more weathered than the last, as if you are walking backward through geological time. By the bottom, the stonework has given way to raw, uncarved cavern. The air is ancient -- it tastes of mineral dust and something floral that has no business existing this far underground. Ahead, gray light filters from a source that should not exist.

hazards.name: Crumbling stairs (Dexterity save on final steps)

event_triggers: On entry: Your torch gutters. The gray light ahead is not fire, not magic -- it is ambient, as if the air itself remembers what daylight was.

content_hints.description: A forest of stone trees fills a cavern half a mile wide. Each tree is petrified -- bark turned to quartz, leaves frozen mid-fall as agate and jasper, roots fused with the bedrock in veins of fossilized wood. Some trees still stand; others lie shattered, their rings exposed like calendar pages. The oldest ring you can count dates this forest to before the founding of Waterdeep. Before the founding of anything. Birds that are not birds perch in the stone branches -- they are stone too, caught mid-song, their beaks open in silence that has lasted millennia.

loot.name: Petrified Wood Branch (arcane focus, worth 50 gp)
Agate Leaves (10 gp each, handfuls available)

event_triggers: On investigation: One petrified tree bears carvings in a language that predates Common, Dwarvish, and Elvish. A Comprehend Languages spell translates it as: 'We were here before. We will be here after. Remember us.'

content_hints.description: A military camp carved into an alcove of the petrified forest. Tents of red dragon hide are arranged in precise rows around a central fire pit that burns without fuel. Githyanki warriors sharpen silver swords that hum with psionic energy, their movements too efficient to be casual -- they are preparing for war. Astral runes ward the camp's perimeter, and a portal shimmer hangs in the air behind the command tent, thin as paper, showing stars that do not belong to any sky on the Material Plane.

npcs.name: Kress'tal
npcs.dialogue: You walk where you do not belong, groundling. This level is contested -- the shadow wyrm claims the deep chambers, and we intend to take them. You may pass, or you may fight beside us. Choose quickly; we do not negotiate with the indecisive.
npcs.notes: Githyanki knight. Scarred, pragmatic, fiercely disciplined. Leading a war party hunting a shadow dragon that lairs deeper in the level. Will accept temporary allies but trusts no one. Psionic abilities make deception nearly impossible.

event_triggers: On alliance: Kress'tal provides tactical information about the shadow dragon's lair and a githyanki war chant that grants resistance to fear for 1 hour.
On hostility: The entire camp mobilizes in 6 seconds. There are more warriors in the tents.

content_hints.description: A coliseum of natural stone, its tiered seats carved from the cavern walls by hands or forces unknown. The arena floor is sand over stone, and the sand is black -- volcanic, imported from somewhere deep. Weapons are embedded in the ground at the arena's center, each one ancient, each one still sharp. The seats are empty, but the impression of an audience lingers -- a pressure in the air, a sense of being watched by thousands of invisible eyes.

loot.name: Ancient Gladiator's Helm (grants advantage on saves vs. fear)

hazards.name: Arena wards (prevent teleportation within the pit)

event_triggers: On weapon drawn in arena: The invisible audience roars. For the duration of combat in the arena, critical hits deal maximum damage.
On victory: The roar becomes a chant. A hidden door in the arena wall grinds open, revealing a passage deeper.

content_hints.description: A stone circle of seven menhirs, each twelve feet tall, each carved with a different rune. The runes pulse in sequence -- a slow, repeating pattern like a heartbeat. At the circle's center, the air is warm and tastes of copper. Objects left inside the circle overnight are changed: iron becomes silver, wood becomes stone, and water becomes a luminous blue fluid that functions as a Potion of Greater Healing. Halaster repurposed something far older than himself, and even he did not fully understand it.

loot.name: Runestone Fragment (arcane focus, +1 to spell attack rolls)

hazards.name: Transmutation field (living creatures save or polymorphed for 1 hour)

event_triggers: On rune study (DC 17 Arcana): The sequence is a countdown. It has been counting down for nine thousand years. It has fourteen iterations remaining.

content_hints.description: The cavern narrows into a passage where shadows move independently of light. They cling to the walls like living things, recoiling from bright light but surging forward in darkness. The temperature drops sharply -- breath fogs, metal frosts, and exposed skin prickles with cold that feels intelligent, targeted, curious. Deep in the passage, two points of violet light hang in the dark, unblinking. They are not torches. They are eyes.

hazards.name: Supernatural darkness (disadvantage on Perception in dim areas)

event_triggers: On light source destroyed: The shadows surge forward. All shadows in the room act immediately.
On passage through: The violet eyes at the end of the corridor blink and withdraw. The shadow dragon knows you are coming.

content_hints.description: A cavern of impossible size, its ceiling lost in darkness that even darkvision cannot penetrate. The floor is covered in a thin layer of ash that muffles footsteps to silence. In the center, coiled around a pillar of obsidian, a dragon made of shadow and hunger opens its eyes. It is not entirely solid -- light passes through it in places, and where its body meets the ground, the ash swirls in patterns that suggest something vast sleeping just beneath the surface. Its breath is not fire but absence -- a cone of nothingness that unmakes light, sound, and hope.

loot.name: Shadow Dragon Scale (5 scales, rare crafting material)
Shadow Dragon's Hoard (1,800 gp in darkened coin)

hazards.name: Living shadow terrain (difficult terrain in darkness)

event_triggers: On combat start: The dragon does not roar. It simply stops pretending to sleep. The ash on the floor rises in a ring around the chamber, and the only exit seals behind a wall of solid darkness.
On githyanki alliance active: Kress'tal and two warriors arrive on round 3, attacking the dragon's flank. 'FOR VLAAKITH! FOR THE ASTRAL SEA!'
On defeat: The dragon dissolves into the ash. The obsidian pillar cracks, revealing a stairway spiraling downward. The shadows in the cavern do not leave -- they watch. They remember.
